questions.question: What is motion?
questions.answer: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time and a reference point.

questions.question: What is meant by a reference point?
questions.answer: A reference point is a fixed point used to describe the position of an object.

questions.question: Define distance.
questions.answer: Distance is the total path length travelled by an object during its motion.

questions.question: Define displacement.
questions.answer: Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions of an object.

questions.question: State one difference between distance and displacement.
questions.answer: Distance is the total path travelled while displacement is the shortest distance between initial and final positions.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.answer: Uniform motion occurs when an object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Define speed.
questions.answer: Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time.

questions.question: What is average speed?
questions.answer: Average speed is the total distance travelled divided by the total time taken.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.answer: The SI unit of speed is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: Define velocity.
questions.answer: Velocity is the speed of an object in a given direction.

questions.question: Define acceleration.
questions.answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of acceleration?
questions.answer: The SI unit of acceleration is metre per second squared (m/s²).

questions.question: What is uniform acceleration?
questions.answer: Uniform acceleration occurs when velocity changes by equal amounts in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What does a distance-time graph represent?
questions.answer: A distance-time graph represents how distance changes with time.

questions.question: What does the slope of a distance-time graph indicate?
questions.answer: The slope of a distance-time graph indicates the speed of the object.

questions.question: What does a velocity-time graph show?
questions.answer: A velocity-time graph shows how velocity changes with time.

questions.question: What does the area under a velocity-time graph represent?
questions.answer: The area under a velocity-time graph represents displacement.

questions.question: Write the first equation of motion.
questions.answer: v = u + at.

questions.question: Write the second equation of motion.
questions.answer: s = ut + ½at².

questions.question: Write the third equation of motion.
questions.answer: v² − u² = 2as.

questions.question: What is uniform circular motion?
questions.answer: Uniform circular motion is the motion of an object along a circular path with constant speed.

questions.question: Give one example of circular motion.
questions.answer: The motion of the Earth around the Sun is an example of circular motion.

questions.question: Name the instrument used to measure distance travelled by a vehicle.
questions.answer: An odometer measures the distance travelled by a vehicle.

questions.question: Name the instrument used to measure speed of a vehicle.
questions.answer: A speedometer measures the speed of a vehicle.
